grades.expectation: Response mentions regularisation as an option (retrospective local authority approval)
grades.reason: Clearly explains regularisation as Option A with detailed pros/cons and process

grades.expectation: Response mentions indemnity insurance as an alternative
grades.reason: Discusses indemnity insurance as Option B with coverage details and limitations

grades.expectation: Response states indemnity insurance must be obtained BEFORE any approach to the local authority
grades.reason: Explicitly states indemnity is only possible if nobody has approached local authority building control and warns not to contact them until strategy is agreed

grades.expectation: Response mentions Part B (fire safety) as a key concern for loft conversions
grades.reason: Repeatedly emphasizes Part B fire safety as critical issue, mentions protected escape routes, fire doors, smoke alarms

grades.expectation: Response provides approximate costs for at least one option (regularisation £400-800 or insurance £50-200)
grades.reason: States indemnity insurance costs often ~£50-£200, though doesn't provide regularisation cost range
